entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q1 ’25)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are pleased to share further updates and maintain the momentum of delivering relevant insights to our community.

In this installment of our blog series, discover the latest Integration packages published on SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners during Q1 2025, along with a high-level summary of the year so far.

For complete details on each partner's content, refer to the partners listing which is available on the home page of SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KaTe GmbH

Integration package published:

KaTe RESTful Adapter

The KaTe RESTful Adapter enables you to use Open APIs with SAP Integration Suite with a simplified development approach. It features the same set of capabilities as our existing RESTful adapter for SAP Process Orchestration and allows you to use SAP Cloud Integration as full featured Open API client or server.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NTT DATA Business Solutions AG

Integration package published:

SAP LeanIX Integration with SAP Business Technology Platform

This integration package is designed to synchronize the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) Account Structure (Global and Subaccounts) with SAP LeanIX, automatically creating Platform Factsheets in SAP LeanIX based on SAP BTP best practices for account structuring.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration package published:

Anaplan Integration with SAP Ariba

Anaplan Integration with SAP Ariba package facilitates seamless data transfer between Anaplan and SAP Ariba to automate the creation of Purchase Requisitions. The integration enables Anaplan to send structured procurement data to SAP Ariba, ensuring efficient and accurate requisition processing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details. 

Stay tuned for further updates and information by the end of the next quarter!

entry.title: Add Value Help with SAP Build Actions (free project)
entry.content:
Value help is one of the key features that can make a UI really friendly. Here's how to do it without much work, using the new integration of Actions with SAP Build Apps.

Even if you do not need value help, you can learn from this project:

  • Actions (including custom $filter)
  • 🆕 New page variables based on existing schema feature
  • Custom events
  • Popups (including showing/hiding and absolute positioning)
  • Layouting and positioning 
  • Events related to input box
  • 🆕 Integrating actions into SAP Build Apps 
  • Working with destinations to retrieve S/4HANA data
  • Paging and filtering

As always we will use the A_BusinessPartner entity so the user can easily select a business partner. The app has 2 ways to provide the value help:

🟪From within an Input Box:

🟪From another screen that lists all the business partners, using paging and filtering:

NOTE: This technique is good for an OData v2 service. You can do the same for a v4 service, though there you could use the CONTAINS keyword in the action or even skip the action and use a "Get record collection" to do the filtering work, since for v4 services this flow function provides the contains filtering option.

Much of the idea for this UI is based on a blog by @botazhat and her blog How to add a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps. I have enhanced it by using actions, and making it work with v2 services and taking data directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

Create Action for Getting Help Values

I think it is better design to use an action to get the help values since it.

  • Hides the API calls from the app developer
  • Hides the filtering syntax, which can be complex and different between OData v2 and v4
  • Makes the front-end development simpler

You can download the project, though it is quite simple to create yourself.

All you have to do to get started is create an action based on the BusinessPartner API service, and use the simple GET all business partners call from the A_BusinessPartner entity.

You will make a few small changes to make it easier to work with:

  • Add filterString as an input parameter to get a string to search in the business partner name.
  • Set defaults for some of the OData query parameters:
    • Set $top to 20, to retrieve only 20 matches at once (this can be overridden by the app)
    • Set $orderby to sort by BusinessPartnerFullName is ascending order (but you can change this)
    • Set $inlinecount to allpages, to return the total number of records, for the case where we paginate through the values.

For the filtering, we set the $filter input. Click the row, which opens the side panel. Then in the side panel, click to open the expression editor.

This will let you you set the $filter clause, either with a GUI or manually by typing in whatever you want.

This is the expression I used, to search for all matches on the BusinessPartnerFullName field using the value in filterString, and eliminating all business partners with no name. Note in v4 we would have had simpler syntax with the CONTAINS keyword.

substringof('${filterString}',BusinessPartnerFullName) eq true and BusinessPartnerFullName ne ''

Release and publish the action.

 

The Project

You can download the project we used to see how the UI, variables and logic. To use it, you'll have to connect your own deployed action. I've tried to make it as generic as possible.

There 2 pages, each providing a different way to get value help:

  • Home Page: Contains the form for business partner ID and name, plus the dropdown to search for a specific business partner.
  • Business Partners: Contains a list of all business partners that you can paginate through or search through.

And we have 2 app variables to store the business partner ID and name.

UI for Dropdown Help

For both pages, we create an integration to the action, and to the destination to be used with the action. Notice that here we create an integration to a destination without enabling any entities -- this was a little weird to me the first time I saw it. 

For variables, we have these:

  • Action_In and Action_Out are created automatically with the new feature that allows you to create variables from an action's schema.
  • filterString is the search string.
  • events is a way to control the updates when someone types fast and reduce the number of calls to the backend (write a comment if you want to learn more). 

And the logic is pretty simple with only 3 main flow functions (skipping all the flow functions related to the events variable):

  1. We capture when someone types in the search field (EVENT)
  2. We ignore when the search string is less than 3 characters (IF CONDITION)
  3. We then run the action (TRIGGER ACTION)
  4. We set the variable to hold the returned values (SET PAGE VARIABLE)

We also added some simple logic to handle when the user leaves the search box or presses escape (clear the value help) or when the user returns (run the original logic above with custom event). 

As for the UI, the main thing is the container holding the value help is absolutely positioned so it aligns with the input box:

And its visibility is set only when there are options to display:

UI for Selecting from All Values

Instead of using a dropdown, we added an icon at the end of the input box that navigates to a different page for selecting the business partner. 

This takes us to a separate page where we display all the business partners, and we can use filtering or paging to find the record we want.

This is the only logic we have, on the page logic canvas (except for clicking one of the business partners and returning to the home page).

When we come to the page we load all the business partners, and we have a custom event to refresh the page whenever we need to.

Then we have 3 flows when different page variables change:

  • User changes search string: We reset the page number to 1 and the OData $skip parameter to 0, and call the backend again.
  • User navigates to a different page: We recalculate the OData $skip parameter, and call the backend again.
  • System changes the # of retrieved records: We recalculate the number of pages we have. This occurs basically whenever we call the backend.

 

Room for Improvement

There are small things we could have done to make it even better:

  • Put the icon for the full business partner list inside the input box, making it a custom component (something Bota did in her blog)
  • Add multi-selection and wildcard characters
  • Package it to make it more generic for any data entity

Also, I wanted to eliminate flashing when the user enters a search string, and there was a tradeoff between the flashing and the speed. I might change my calculus to make it faster.

I'll work on these. In the meantime, tell me what you thought of these techniques, including how you would improve them.

😸

entry.title: SAP Principal Propagation without Secrets: How Managed Identity in APIM Simplifies Everything
entry.content:
Dear community,

SAP Principal Propagation (for simplicity often also referred to as SSO) is the gold standard for app integration – especially when it comes to 3rd party apps such as Microsoft Power Platform. Building on top of my prior blog on API Management usage for SAP SSO, I am sharing today how you can fully eliminate the passwords or certificates for the token exchange previously required.

No more password storing or rotation. No certificates to renew. No manual key rollover scripts.

Thank god!

The Microsoft Entra ID authentication handshakes with services such as SAP BTP, SuccessFactors, Azure, or M365 apps like Power Platform allows these apps to become native, dynamic, and self-maintaining.

This shift not only tightens security by reducing the attack surface but also significantly accelerates project delivery, improves compliance with modern Zero Trust principles, and cuts down on administrative overhead. It’s a small architectural change with a big real-world impact — and a logical next step for enterprise-grade app integrations in the SAP ecosystem.

 

A glimpse under the hood

The magic? It’s called Managed Identity for Azure. With a user-assigned managed identity in API Management, the platform takes care of the authentication for you — clean, automatic, and secret-free. Building on top of Workload Identity Federation enables using a managed identity as a credential, just like certificate or password, on Entra ID Applications.

See following updated flow diagram from the prior blog. It highlights with red and yellow arrows where it was possible to “eliminate” credentials.

When browsing Microsoft Learn or additional blogs, look for the technical term “Federated Identity Credential” for additional details.

Solving this challenge on Azure API Management level enables scaling the approach to arbitrary many different consumer solutions.

Find the updated policy on the Azure API Management repos for SAP ECC and S/4HANA here and for SuccessFactors here.

Learn more about this space in general from the blog series by my magnificent colleague Martin Raepple.

 

Create a user-assigned managed identity for your Azure API Management instance

 

See additional details on this Microsoft Learn article.

 

Create a “managed credential”

This step is being referred to by Microsoft Learn as Federated Identity Credential (FIC).

  • Click Add Credential as per below screenshot from the manage secrets pane of your Entra ID app registration which represents your API Management instance.
  • Choose scenario “Managed Identity”,
  • Select the id of your user-assigned-managed identity created before, and
  • Make sure the audience is listed as “api://AzureADTokenExchange”.

 

Import the updated policy

Navigate to your SAP OData API on Azure API Management and use the Azure portal UI to paste the policy as code, or leverage infrastructure-as-code with your devops pipeline to apply SAP Principal Propagation at scale. See this Azure Developer CLI sample using Bicep for reference.

See the APIM policy line doing the magical managed identity request. It is as simple as that:

<authentication-managed-identity resource="api://azureadtokenexchange" client-id="{{APIMUserAssignedManagedIdentityId}}" output-token-variable-name="msi-access-token" ignore-error="false" />

The rest happens under the hood.

Learn more about policy creation including Microsoft Copilot usage from this article and this video. In case you fancy GitHub Copilot apply the VS Code extension. See this community post for further inspiration.

Consider the governance and security trade-offs between mighty policies and SAP OAuth clients authorized for everything vs. a multitude of clients for each API and scope.

 

Configure the policy using named values

The configuration exercise is 99% the same as outlined in the earlier blog “SAP OData connector now supports OAuth2 and SAP Principal Propagation” before. However, now we replace the client secret setting for the Entra ID app registration that represents your API Management instance with the client id of your user-assigned managed identity.

 

Fill the values as per your environment:

Key

Value sample

Hints

AADTenantId

12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz

Find it on the Azure portal (e.g. here)

APIMAADRegisteredAppClientId

999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The Application (client) id of the Entra ID app registration representing your Azure API Management instance.

APIMUserAssignedManagedIdentityId

7777abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

Client ID of user-assigned managed identity of APIM instance

AADSAPResource

https://a4h100

The provider’s name from your NetWeaver SAML setup. Typically, a URL with SID followed Client number

sap-oauth-client-username

ODATAOAUTH

User name provided on SOAUTH2 transaction (/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/oauth2_config?sap-client=100)

 

Create a named user on SU01 with minimum rights (S_SCOPE, S_SERVICE) and reference that on SOAUTH2.

Don’t forget to assign authorized scope.

sap-oauth-client-password

Password for that oauth user

This is only used to request tokens not to authenticate to SAP.

sap-oauth-scope

ZPRODUCTSVIEW_CDS_0001

The scope assigned on SOAUTH2

SAPOAuthRefreshExpiry

86400

Option to set refresh token expiry (default on SAP 2 years). It is not part of the token response.

SAPOAuthServerAdressForTokenEndpoint

a4h-internal.cloudapp.net:44301

Host and port of the target SAP OAuth server. When SAP cloud connector is used, put the virtual hostname and port.

Hints on troubleshooting

There was good feedback on the Postman collection to verify proper SAP Principal Propagation setup. Be aware that the on-behalf-of flow step to request the SAML assertion from Entra involving the managed identity cannot be tested via a REST client from your local device, because only Azure services are trusted for the request.

In general, I would recommend testing the auth flow with the least components involved as possible to reduce problem surface. Meaning, test with a REST client of your choice with client secrets to make sure that your SAP SSO setup and OAuth settings are working. Keep the API Management part and managed identity to after successful initial integration with SAP.

 

The wider integration context

Q: Does Managed Identity for Azure work only for SAP on Azure?

A: No, using Azure API Managment self-hosted Gateway you can bring that capability any-premises. Besides deploying the gateway directly into your local network, consider also Azure  public cloud routing options (ExpressRoute, NVAs, VPN, etc.)

Q: SAP Cloud Connector already supports Principal Propagation for on-premises. Why would I choose this managed identity approach?

A: SAP Integration Suite using SAP Cloud Connector for instance is a prime choice - well established and mature. Nothing wrong with that. Still the credentials need to be managed. You may consider a hybrid where Integration Suite realizes connectivity, while Managed Identity for Azure provides passwordless token requests. Be aware to avoid configuring Principal Propagation twice (OAuth2SAMLBearer flow vs. the Cloud Connector setting with short-lived x.509 certs)

 

Thoughts on production readiness

Managed Identities are a well-established platform feature on Azure with large scale adoption across services. Furthermore, it is the recommended way of configuring service to service authentication.

Credentials remain in place for the SAP OAuth server as per its OAuth2 flow capabilities. Halfway there so to say. Luckily, token users have a much lower risk profile than actual users.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap 🌯. Today you saw how SAP Principal Propagation goes secret-less on Microsoft Entra ID — no passwords, and no certificates. Entra ID with Managed Identity handles it all, mapping Microsoft identities to SAP users while keeping your authorizations intact.

This is what native, secure integration should look like.

👉If you’re still managing credentials manually in 2025, it’s time for an upgrade. It only gets worse from here: Certificate authorities agreed to reduce lifespan to 47 days in stages over the next 1-3 years.

Cheers Martin

entry.title: Future of SAP BTP Copilot + Joule + Custom AI to Trigger Actions After Meetings
entry.content:
In most companies, meetings end and… nothing happens. Important insights are lost, action items are forgotten, and follow-ups are delayed.

That’s why we created Júlio, a lightweight assistant that reads meeting transcripts, interprets them using GPT, and triggers real actions — like sending emails or starting SAP Build Process Automation workflows.

 

🔧 What I Built:

A functional prototype that connects:

- A fake Copilot that detects when a meeting ends.
- A fake Joule, SAP’s digital assistant, triggered by the meeting event.
- Our custom assistant, Júlio, which receives the meeting transcript and extracts actionable items using GPT.
- A final automation layer — either sending emails or interacting with SAP ERP workflows directly.

Here’s a video showing the full process in action:
👉 [YouTube link here]
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BNfK0xKXfx0

💡 Why This Matters

This prototype demonstrates how SAP tools like Joule can be extended with external intelligence and automation — turning meeting insights into real business actions.

You can imagine a future where Joule + Copilot become the standard interface for triggering purchase orders, creating sales quotations, or logging follow-ups — all based on natural language.

🤝 Let’s Collaborate!

We’d love feedback, ideas, and partners who want to push this idea further.

Drop your thoughts in the comments or reach out if you want to help evolve this into a real-world SAP extension. Let’s build something amazing together!

entry.title: Ciao everyone and a big welcome to the new SAP Community Group – Rome
entry.content:
I’m super happy that Rome finally has its own local group in the SAP Community and I really hope lots of you will join! 🎉
Sono davvero contenta che anche Roma abbia finalmente il suo spazio nella community… e spero che in tanti decideranno di farne parte!

This is our place to stay in touch between events, share ideas, plan meetups or just have some relaxed conversations.
Qui possiamo tenerci in contatto tra un evento e l’altro, scambiarci idee, organizzare qualcosa insieme o semplicemente fare due chiacchiere in libertà.

Feel free to start a discussion on anything SAP topics, ideas for the next SAP Stammtisch Rome, thoughts for a SAP Inside Track, or even just to say hi! 🙂
Sentitevi liberi di iniziare nuove conversazioni: spunti per eventi, domande, curiosità… oppure anche solo per salutarci!

This group is informal and open to everyone the more voices, the more fun! 😊
È un gruppo aperto, informale, dove tutti sono i benvenuti... più siamo, meglio è!

I’ve been part of the SAP Community for many years, and I’m really happy that we finally have a local group here in Italy’s capital!
My hope is to bring together people from Rome and beyond, anyone who’s interested in connecting, sharing ideas, and helping this group grow. Whether you’re new or experienced, you’re more than welcome to join us. Let’s make some noise together!

Faccio parte della SAP Community da tantissimi anni, e sono davvero felice che finalmente abbiamo un gruppo locale qui nella capitale.
Il mio desiderio è riuscire a riunire persone da Roma e da tutta Italia, chiunque abbia voglia di conoscersi, condividere idee e far crescere insieme questo gruppo. Che siate nuovi o parte della community da tempo, siete più che benvenuti. Facciamo casino insieme!

Can’t wait to chat with you all and see what we’ll build together here in Rome!
Non vedo l’ora di conoscervi e vedere cosa riusciremo a creare insieme qui a Roma!

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025:Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community!

My name is Joanne Koh-Stewart, and I am Director of Product Marketing for SAP BTP for Business Applications and Core. I focus on identifying and nurturing SAP BTP use cases that solve real customer challenges across the Business Suite and lines of business. You might have seen my name around Sapphire and TechEd times, writing about our SAP BTP use cases.

I'm pleased to announce that I'll be presenting again at this year's SAP Sapphire Orlando. My session Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today with my colleague @denisema, is scheduled for Tuesday, May 20th at 11:30am – 11:50am EDT.

 

What you will learn about:

This session is particularly exciting because it allows me to share insights gained from our customers and partners who leveraged SAP BTP. The idea for this session emerged from numerous conversations with customers eager to understand what they could do with SAP BTP and how they could quickly implement it to address their pressing business challenges. We've curated a selection of use cases that demonstrate tangible value and can be rapidly deployed.  

For customers who want to try out SAP BTP but are not ready to commit, this session will dive into how customers have different options to get started for free with SAP BTP.

 

We look forward to seeing you:

Whether you're well-versed in SAP BTP or just beginning to explore its potential, this session promises valuable takeaways on business challenges that SAP BTP can help resolve. Denise and I look forward to meeting you at the session or at the BTP for Business Application booth at SAP Sapphire Orlando, and engaging in insightful discussions about the transformative power of SAP BTP and how it can address your specific business needs. Make sure to add Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today session to your Favourites to add it to your Sapphire agenda. 

 

Other related sessions I recommend:  

Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center - BTP2467 

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic: 

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Build
  • SAP Business Suite
  • SAP Integration Suite

Related community topic pages: 

  •  SAP Business Technology Platform In Action

 

Now let's hear from you:

  • How are you currently driving digital transformation within your organization?
  • What are your main goals for using SAP BTP?
  • What are/were your biggest challenges in getting started with SAP BTP?

Tell us in the comments, and we look forward to seeing you virtual and/or in Orlando in a few weeks!

entry.title: SAP LeanIX integrating Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps
entry.content:
When Enterprise Architects and IT Security Speak the Same Language

This blog was co-authored by @Michelle10 (PM @ SAP LeanIX)

Dear community,

In most enterprises, IT security and enterprise architecture are two critical disciplines, each with their own priorities, tools, and perspectives. One is laser-focused on threat detection, compliance, and access control. The other is mapping capabilities, rationalizing applications, and shaping long-term IT strategy.

What happens when these personas complement each other – not just in theory, but in tooling?

That’s exactly where the integration between SAP LeanIX and Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps comes in.

But wait, what about my managed apps governed by my corporate identity provider? No worries, SAP LeanIX may be integrated with Microsoft Entra ID for discovery and keeping track which apps have SSO etc. as well.

 

Connecting Two Worlds

Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is your frontline for SaaS security and governance to detect threat attacks that arise from your SaaS app interactions and reduce the exposure to such attacks. Furthermore, it helps security teams uncover shadow IT, assess risk, and monitor data flows across

SAP LeanIX, meanwhile, gives enterprise architects the visibility they need to manage complexity – mapping applications to business capabilities, tracking lifecycle data, and supporting IT transformation.

The magic happens when you combine them.

 

 

From Discovery to Decision-Making

With this integration, you can pull application usage and risk data from Defender for Cloud Apps directly into SAP LeanIX. That means:

Security teams discover new or risky SaaS apps…

…Architects contextualize those apps within business functions and processes.

 You’re no longer just identifying what’s out there - you’re aligning it with your architecture strategy. This turns ad-hoc discovery into intentional governance.

 

Business Outcomes

  • Eliminate shadow IT with traceable business context.
  • Support rationalization with usage-driven decisions.
  • Align security and strategy with shared data and mutual visibility.

 

Why It Matters

This integration fosters collaboration between two personas that often operate in parallel but rarely intersect. With Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps and SAP LeanIX, they do - bringing security and architecture into a shared conversation around risk, capability, and value.

The result? Better decisions, stronger governance, and a much clearer view of your SaaS landscape. All powered from the Defender and Entra ID APIs. Not too bad, huh?

This is how modern IT operates: cross-functional, data-driven, and strategically aligned.

 

Get Started

Anyone curious about uncovering shadow IT and making better SaaS app decisions? Just reach out to me and Michelle or leave a comment.

 

Cheers

Martin & Michelle

______________

References:

  • Defender for Cloud Apps REST API | Microsoft Learn
  • Microsoft Defender for Cloud apps API in Microsoft Graph | Microsoft Learn
  • Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (MDCA) Integration for SaaS Discovery | LeanIX User Docs
  • Microsoft Entra ID Integration for SaaS Discovery | LeanIX User Docs

entry.title: Sapphire Orlando&Madrid 2025: World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, My name is Joschua Goetz and I'm an IT Technology Services Senior Specialist, working in a very special project about esports. Together with our Partner Team Liquid we build esports analytics dashboards to boost team and player performance.

I’m excited to participate at SAP Sapphire Orlando and Madrid this year, where I’ll be joined by Team Liquid to talk about our awesome partnership and how we use analytics to help them win.

My Sessions Overview

Orlando:
World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software - DIS2157 

Here i have the honor to present together with Thom Valks Partnerships Manager at Team Liquid. Our session is at 3:30 PM - 3:50 PM EDT on May 20. 

Madrid:
World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software - DIS1454 

In Madrid i'll be on stage with Jason Lucas Luijckx Director of Partnerships at Team Liquid. Our session is scheduled at 11:30 AM - 11:50 AM CEST on May 28.

What you will learn about

The thrilling intersection of esports and innovative technology as you join us to experience the transformative power of SAP software in competitive gaming. Learn how our internal IT helps leading esports organization Team Liquid with SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP AI Core, Joule, and SAP Business Technology Platform.

Looking forward to seeing you

The world of esports is a data rich, high stakes world of competitive gaming. With SAP software it is possible to gain that ever so small edge over your opponents. Curious how we did it with Team Liquid ?

Register here for our SAP Sapphire sessions in Orlando or Madrid

Not able to attend SAP Sapphire but still curious ? Learn more about our partnership here SAP x Team Liquid 

Now let’s hear from you

Did you know that SAP was involved in esports ? 
If you play DOTA2 or League of Legends, what do you think could be some of the most influential parts of the game we should look at ?
Share your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: Participate in the Documentation Feedback Survey for SAP Job Scheduling Service
entry.content:
Are you using the SAP Job Scheduling service? Have you ever had a hard time understanding the concepts and processes described in the documentation on SAP Help Portal?

Your insights are invaluable to us! We invite you to participate in our Documentation Feedback Survey, available at https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling. Your feedback will help us enhance the quality and usability of our documentation, ensuring it meets your needs and expectations.

 

Why Your Feedback Matters

Documentation is a crucial resource for users navigating the SAP Job Scheduling service. It provides guidance, troubleshooting tips, and detailed explanations of features. However, we understand that documentation can always be improved. Your feedback will help us identify areas that need clarity, additional examples, or even new sections.

 

How to Participate

1. Head over to https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling.

2. Choose Give Feedback.

3. Share your thoughts with us. Let us know what you find helpful, what could be improved, and any specific suggestions you have.

Your input directly influences the updates and enhancements we make to our documentation.

 

What We Aim to Achieve

By gathering feedback from you, we aim to create documentation that is not only informative but also intuitive and user-friendly. Whether you're a seasoned SAP user or new to job scheduling, your perspective is crucial in shaping the resources we provide.

 

Thank you for taking the time to help us improve. Your participation is a step towards better, more effective documentation for everyone. We look forward to hearing from you!

entry.title: Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP using OAuth2 Authentication
entry.content:
1. Introduction
This guide provides a detailed step-by-step procedure to configure the SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) Mail Adapter to send emails using Outlook SMTP with OAuth2 authentication.
2. Prerequisites
Before proceeding, ensure you have the following:
• Access to an SAP CPI tenant
• Outlook account with access to Azure AD (Azure
Active Directory)
• Administrator privileges in Azure AD
• Client ID and Client Secret for OAuth2
authentication
• Access to the SAP CPI Web UI

3. Create a New App Registration in Azure AD
• Step 1: Register a new app in Azure Active
Directory.

  • Go to the Azure Portal: portal.azure.com.

Scroll down and find App resistration -

Navigate to Azure Active Directory > App Registrations

> New Registration.

 

Click on App resistration -

Create app > Fill details -

Fill in the following fields:

  • Name: Enter a name for the app (e.g., "SAP CPI Mail Adapter").
  • Supported account types: Select Accounts in this organizational directory only.
  • Redirect URI: Set the URI to

https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient (or another relevant URI as needed).

Click Register.

App is Created –

Step 2: Get the Client ID and Tenant ID

  1. After the registration, copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID. These are required for authentication.

     

    Step 3: Generate a Client Secret
  2. Go to Certificates & Secrets in the app registration.
  3. Under Client secrets, click New client secret.
  4. Add a description (e.g., "SAP CPI Mail Secret") and set the expiration.
  5. Click Add and copy the Value of the client secret. This is your Client Secret.

Create New +

Copy Secret value it will be used at the time of creating OAuth 2 Authentication Code configuration

5.   Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter

Step 1: Navigate to the SAP CPI Web UI

  1. Go to the SAP CPI tenant.
  2. Open the Integration Flow (iFlow) that you want to configure for sending emails.

Step : Set up Mail Adapter

  1. Add the Mail Adapter to your iFlow.
  2. In the Mail Adapter settings:
    • Set SMTP Server to smtp.office365.com.
    • Set Port to 587.
    • Set Security to STARTTLS.

Step 3: Configure OAuth2 Authentication

  1. In the Authentication Type dropdown, select OAuth2.
  2. Configure the OAuth2 Authorization Code Credential with the following details:
    • Authorization URL:

https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize

 

  • Token URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token

 

  • Client ID: Enter the Application (client) ID from the Azure AD registration.

 

  • Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret you generated in Azure AD.

 

  • Scope:

Enter https://graph.microsoft.com/.default.

 

  • https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send ofline_access

 

Fill all fileds –

Grant Permissions: Ensure that the Outlook account has the necessary permissions to send emails (via Mail.Send).

Add These permissions-

6.   Troubleshooting

Common Errors and Solutions

  • Error: AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application
    • Solution: Ensure that the admin consent is granted in Azure AD.
  • Error: SendAsDeniedException
    • Solution: Ensure that the correct account is being used to send emails and that permissions are set correctly in Azure AD.

7.   Conclusion

  • Now you have successfully configured the SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP using OAuth2 authentication. This setup enables secure email communication through the Outlook service, using the recommended OAuth2 protocol.

Appendix

  • Important URLs:
    • Azure AD Portal
    • SAP CPI Documentation
    • Microsoft Graph API

NOTE –

These Can be done with only Administrative account of MS 365 only and for both accounts Business Account (@company.com or @School.com) and Personal Account (@outlook.com) settings are different!

Check Authentication for the Email -

 For Personal account if you are a free user then you have to change it manually after this from manifest !

 

Thanks,
Dushyant

entry.title: SAP Datasphere News in April
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere News in April

Another month with three new releases is in the books ✨

We delivered great new features in SAP Datasphere with all April releases. Learn more in my short top features video in April 2025 and the quarterly What’s New session on YouTube 📺

In addition, explore the latest updates in our community news blogs.

Check out the new book about SAP BW modernization: Unlocking SAP BW: Evolution from Legacy to Cloud to learn about the different options for your SAP BW environment in combination with SAP Datasphere or SAP Business Data Cloud.

 

 

My top features in April

 

Analytic Model

Stacking

You can create a new analytic model using another analytic model as fact source and re-use the modelled definitions for measures, exposed attributes, dimensions, and variables.
➡️  More information

Replace The Fact Source of an Analytic Model

You can replace the fact source of an existing analytic model by dragging the new fact source onto the fact source of the analytic model on the canvas.
➡️  More information.

Impact and Lineage Analysis for Measures

You can display an impact and lineage analysis for measures in the analytic model.

Format of Measures

You can now change the format of a measure (decimal places and scale) in an analytic model in the Formatting section in the editor.

 

Data Validation: Referential Integrity

A new validation rule helps you ensure that all values in foreign key columns in fact views are present in the key columns of the associated dimensions.
➡️  More information

 

Task chains: Deep Retry

For task chains that include nested task chains, the behavior for retrying failed tasks within a nested chain has been improved. When a task chain reports a failure, and the failure occurred running one of the tasks inside a nested task chain, retry of the parent task chain no longer requires rerunning all the tasks in the nested task chain, but only needs to rerun tasks from the point where an individual task reported a failure.
➡️  More information

 

Microsoft SQL Server as source in Replication Flow

You can now use connection type Microsoft SQL Server as a source to replicate data from these Microsoft SQL Server databases to all targets in replication flows.
➡️  More information

 

 

 

Quarterly What’s New in SAP Datasphere

We just ran our quarterly What's New in SAP Datasphere session on YouTube on April 29th, 2025! Get the latest updates on features and functions delivered in Q1 2025.

The session covers the following topics 👇
🔹️  General Product Updates
🔹️  Updates in Data Integration
🔹️  Updates in Data Modeling
🔹️  Updates on various other topics
🔹️  Outlook

👉 Watch the recording https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jq2oDD40uyQ

 

 

SAPPHIRE

It is already May and that is SAP SAPPHIRE time. We also have great coverage with more than 30 sessions on SAP Business Data Cloud including SAP Datasphere by product managers, executives, customers, and partners covering all areas of BDC including roadmap sessions, demos, and strategy talks. Customers and partners will share their experience and if you are keen on chatting with experts drop by our booth area with SAP Business Data Cloud. We are also offering first hands-on experience as part of the ASUG pre-conference in case you want to get your hands on it.

🔗  ASUG pre-conference seminar - Mastering SAP BTP: Drive Digital Transformation with Hands-on Experience - ASUG101
🔗  SAP Business Data Cloud track on the session catalog

 

More blogs to check out 👇

  • SAP Datasphere CLI & Python: Exporting Modeling Objects to CSV Files for Each Artifact
  • SAP Datasphere - Data flow scripts and generic OData - Unpacking nested values
  • A demo to consume MySQL database in Datasphere with Generic JDBC connection
  • SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: An Architect’s guide: SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Databricks - BDC263
  • SAP Datasphere External Access Overview: APIs, CLI and SQL
  • Building Intelligent Data Applications with SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine
  • Seamless Planning with Datasphere & SAC: Seamless Planning in the Cloud?
  • Get Hands-On Workshops with SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Business Data Cloud : Expert Guided Implementation series
  • SAP Business Data Cloud - A Complete Multi-Architecture Platform?
  • Managing planning across global and local structures with SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Run APL on SAP Data Products of Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Business Data Cloud and what it means for SAP Datasphere
  • 3 Reasons to Register for the Modernizing SAP BW with SAP Business Data Cloud Webinar
  • Generic OData Service - Datasphere Integration
  • Data Doesn’t Drive the Business - Trust Does. SAP Business Data Cloud Delivers
  • Access SAP HANA Cloud Database Underneath of SAP Datasphere with a Database user
  • Automation Alone Is Not a Strategy: Here’s Why
  • ETL Objects in SAP Datasphere and Dataflows Example
  • SAP Business Data Fabric: Unifying Your Enterprise Data with BDC (Business Data Cloud)
  • Three Reasons SAP BusinessObjects Still Matters in 2025
  • The Next Decade of Enterprise Technology: Are We Ready?
  • Data Access Control in Data Builder and Authorization Scenario in Business Builder - SAP Datasphere 2
  • Data Access Control in Data Builder and Authorization Scenario in Business Builder - SAP Datasphere 1

 

Find more information and related blog posts on the topic page for SAP Datasphere. You will find further product information on our Community with various subpages about Business Content, the SAP BW Bridge as well as content for Best Practices & Troubleshooting. Also check out the new support content for SAP Datasphere on SAP Help for troubleshooting and analysis guides, how-to guides, technical details, and more.

Find out how to unleash the power of your business data with SAP’s free learning content on SAP Datasphere. It’s designed to help you enrich your data projects, simplify the data landscape, and make the most out of your investment. Check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on  SAP Learning site.

entry.title: SAPPhire Orlando 2025: How agentic AI revolutionizes the way SAP is running support
entry.content:
My name is Michèle Weynandt and I’m Head of Product Management for Service Management Processes and Solutions. I'm supporting SAP’s digital transformation in after-sales and cloud operations across all lines of business at SAP impacting over 35,000 employees globally. My priorities reside in infusing all processes (customer service management, it service management as well as it operations management) with generative AI, as well as unlocking the power of agentic AI in this area by leveraging existing platforms at SAP including their data.  

My Session: How agentic AI revolutionizes the way SAP is running support, May 20, 2025 11:30 AM-11:50 AM EDT Session ID DIS1727

What you will learn about: In this session we will showcase how we’ve designed our first code based agentic scenario for customer support. We will showcase the business problem, the use case design as well as a short demo.

Looking forward to seeing you: I’m looking forward to seeing you to share with you our thoughts on a flexible and scalable architecture, a demo of the scenario as well as sharing lessons learned so far.

Now let’s hear from you - please feel free to share your thoughts/expectations/insights in the comments! 🚀

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Future-proof your integration investment with AI (BTP2121)
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, My name is Arti Gopalan and I am a lead product manager based out of India for SAP Integration Suite, which is an enterprise-grade integration platform-as-a-service that helps businesses seamlessly connect and integrate their applications, data, and processes.

My session: Strategy Talk - Future-proof your integration investment with AI - BTP2121 on May 20th, 4:00 PM - 4:20 PM EDT as part of the SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando.

What you will learn about: 

As someone deeply involved in the evolution of enterprise integration at SAP, I am excited to be at SAP Sapphire once again this year to represent SAP Integration Suite. You can meet me at the booth – Move from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite - BTP2786. Location: Platform, 157. I will also be presenting a strategy talk on the innovation areas being focused by our team to future-proof your integration investments.

There is an ever-evolving demand for interconnected ecosystems. Ensuring that applications, systems, and processes are connected and are able to respond in real-time has become table stakes across organizations. There is a focused need to support agile integrations and accelerate responsiveness that allows adapting to the dynamic business requirements and the constant influx of new technologies. Discovering process inefficiencies, automating enterprise-wide processes and accelerating innovations across the organization and beyond have become the drivers.

In this session, I will be providing an in-depth look the how we are leveraging Artificial Intelligence to augment the core capabilities of SAP Integration Suite. I'll be illustrating how the intelligent application of AI is poised to fundamentally reshape how we approach integration, leading to significant gains in developer productivity and operational efficiencies through:

  • Intelligent Assistance in Integration Design
  • AI-Driven Transformation and Mapping
  • Optimizing existing integrations
  • Automated Anomaly Detection, Remediation and Trend Prediction

If you're responsible for the critical task of designing, implementing, and maintaining resilient and scalable integration solutions, this session will be interesting for you. I look forward to a rich and engaging discussion.

Other related sessions I recommend:  

See all SAP Integration Suite sessions available to you in Orlando: customer use cases, strategy talks, road map reviews and much more is in store for you - do not miss it!

Browse the catalog & register ➡️ https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/flow/sap/so25/catalog-inperson/page/catalog?tab.sessionplanned=1692641568884001cZ9d&search=%22SAP%20Integration%20Suite%22

Now let’s hear from you: 

What specific capability or future direction of SAP Integration Suite are you most eager to explore and would like to hear more about? Please answer by adding a comment in the comments section.

Register for SAP Sapphire 2025 and join me at the session BTP2121: Future-proof your integration investment with AI. I'm eager to connect with you there.

See you soon.

entry.title: Agentic AI Evaluation: Observability, Traceability & Metrics That Matter
entry.content:
Introduction

Today, enterprises are creating agents, either to automate some manual processes or to optimize existing once. No matter what is the use case, we have already entered the Economy of Agents. This is similar to last several decades where software engineers, developers or consultants developed production grade applications.

Software development evolved over decades which gave us some time to adapt to it and accept new solutions/frameworks. Also the pace with which we were developing applications was slow as compared to the pace with which agentic solutions are being developed.

The critical difference between traditional software solutions/applications and agentic solutions is the very nature. Traditional software solutions are deterministic in nature as opposed to agentic solutions which are non deterministic in nature to an extent.

Quality Gate for traditional software development has evolved over years of iterations and given us some robust test automation tools and frameworks. The harsh truth is - we still find production bugs.

Now, imagine a non deterministic agentic software application for which testing is hard to define or predict at times. This may lead to totally unpredictable production bugs or unexpected behaviour. This might cost us time and money, that too - exponentially.

That is the reason, evaluation of Agentic solutions is very important and should be considered top priority for enterprises to ensure we produce good quality of Agentic applications.

This blog is not to scare you, but to give you a spark to think and act.

So, gear up! and let's start 🚀

I will breakdown the article into following parts

  1. What is agent evaluation?
  2. Types of agent evaluation
  3. Evaluation Tools
  4. Evaluation framework (Block Diagram)
  5. Why OpenTelemetry?
  6. Code walkthrough
  7. Available Agentic Evaluation frameworks

What is Agent Evaluation?

Agent evaluation is validating the output of an agent. This differs slightly from the typical software development testing. In a software testing, we know what is a input and what is the expected output.

But in case of agentic solutions, as the response is given by the underlying LLM, the output varies due to lot of factors such as prompts, LLM used etc.

So, agentic AI testing is done in a different way. It is based on some pre-defined metrics such as response times, underlying tools used by the agent, token consumption, cost of total response etc. to name a few.

Based on the evaluation results, the respective developer can tweak the solution either by enhancing the prompts, changing the underlying LLM, introducing additional tool which will reduce the over all response time and token consumption, but also ensuring the expected output is provided.

Agent evaluation is a evolving space and a lot of advancements are being done to streamline it.

Types of Agent Evaluation

  1. Code based evaluation: It is a pro-code type of evaluation and you will get a glimpse of it in this tutorial/POC. In code based evaluation, code is injected in the Agentic AI application which captures the metric data when the agent it executing any request. It is well suited to validate objectivity of the solution. By that, I mean it is well suited to validate the quantitative attributes of the agentic solution, for example - latency, response time, token consumption etc.
  2. LLM as a judge: This is kind of low-code type of evaluation. This is not covered in this tutorial/POC. As the name suggests, here LLM is a reviewer. It validates the correctness of the output provided by the agentic application. It is well suited to validate subjectivity of the solution. By that, I mean it is well suited to validate the qualitative attributes of the agentic solution, for example - correctness of output response, reasoning, planning etc.

Evaluation Tools

Pre-requisite: For running this observability stack on your local machine, you should have docker desktop installed. Once done, install below docker images.
  1. Prometheus: Time series data store for collecting metric data and alerting.
  2. Grafana Tempo: A distributed tracing backend that simplifies storing and visualizing trace data.
  3. OpenTelemetry Collector: Acts as a central hub for receiving traces and exporting telemetry data to observability backends.
  4. Grafana: Grafana is an interactive data visualization and monitoring platform that allows users to query, visualize, and understand data from various source.

Evaluation Framework (Block Diagram)

Figure-1

 

Why OpenTelemetry?

A very obvious question after looking at the above block diagram is "Why OpenTelemetry?"

OpenTelemetry is an open standard for collecting and exporting telemetry data like metrics and traces.

I would summarize in 5 important points:

  1. Vendor neutral and can integrate with any backend for visualization
  2. Provides diverse language support (Python, Node.js etc)
  3. Batch processing which helps to speed up the trace collection
  4. Provides standard format for collection of observability data
  5. Powerful tracing providing parent-child relationship and context propagation

If you want to deep dive into open telemetry, I have added a link in the reference section.

Code Walkthrough

Till now we have discussed the theory and block diagram, now it's time to deep dive in the code. The Agent Evaluation section of the README details out each and every step to help you get started. I would like to mention some important files in this section for you to give extra attention when you are browsing the code

  1. observability folder has all the config files for open telemetry collector, tempo and prometheus.
  2. agent_observability.py - This singleton class is the core of observability. This holds all the methods for capturing the metrics and traces. I have created decorators which are applied on the actual functions to capture traces. Custom attributes like request_size_in_bytes, agent_name, response_time are captured. You can add more attributes here such as token_consumption etc.
  3. agent_service.py - In this file, you will see the decorator applied to the "/chat" endpoint method.
  4. weather_functions.py - In this file, you will see the decorator applied to all the weather functions.

I have applied the tracing only to weather agent, but can be extended to finance agent.

But there are over the shelf evaluation frameworks available which can be leveraged and provide certain level of customizations. The next section talks about it.

Available Agentic Evaluation Frameworks

I have listed few agentic AI evaluations frameworks available and are good starting point to start learning and understanding the importance of agent evaluation.

  1. LangSmith - https://www.langchain.com/langsmith 
  2. Phoenix - https://docs.arize.com/phoenix
  3. DeepEval - https://www.deepeval.com 

Conclusion

Drawing from my experience, I must emphasize that these insights can serve as a starting point, though their applicability may vary based on specific scenarios. This project will surely give you a starting point from design and development of Agentic AI evaluation.

Along with the theory, my intention is to showcase a fully functional prototype which is publicly available on - GitHub

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my experience and learnings about Agentic AI and agent evaluation.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://opentelemetry.io/docs/languages/python/ 
  2. https://grafana.com/blog/2021/05/04/get-started-with-distributed-tracing-and-grafana-tempo-using-foobar-a-demo-written-in-python/ 
  3. https://prometheus.io/ 
  4. https://opentelemetry.io/docs/what-is-opentelemetry/

entry.title: Helpful links for your SAP transformation
entry.content:
The architecture chapter can use and recommend the tools and services below to guide and equip (and create principles and guardrails to) professionals and managers to support evidence-based decisions for your business and digital transformations.

The links below can help to provide visibility and clarity of your SAP ecosystem, especially to non-SAP professionals and leaders, so they can also make better questions when discovering and exploring the myriad of capabilities that SAP can offer.

You will need an SAP S-user (me.sap.com) and an SAP account (sap.com) to access the content.

  • SAP Best Practices based on SAP Signavio Process Navigator, alternatively, SAP Signavio Process Explorer is the gateway to get insights into SAP’s process portfolio and leverage related process implementation guidance and reference business and solution architectures. These include business-capability and solution maps, process models, metrics, industry best practices, and SAP best-practice and product recommendations.

 

 

  • SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to explore, plan and implement SAP Fiori, the latest user experience from SAP including all relevant content for the SAP Fiori launchpad.

 

  • SAP Business Technology Platform to explore and understand different architecture patterns which can be implemented using SAP BTP services.

 

  • SAP BTP Guidance Framework for architects, developers, and administrators.

 

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (or Intelligent Enterprise Reference Solution Architecture) to help discover, consume, and create integrations, extensions, and innovations.

 

  • SAP Activate provides a clear guided methodology to deploy, adopt and extend new capabilities with different approaches, e.g., agile or waterfall, cloud, hybrid or on-premise, implementation or upgrade.

 

  • SAP Road Maps support the journey to SAP’s future product portfolio. Before customising, check for future enhancements.

 

  • SAP Customer Influence offers a range of programs that give customers the opportunity to influence SAP software development decisions, request specific enhancements or features, and adopt new innovations early on. Before customising, check (and vote) for opportunities and ideas to speed up SAP’s development and delivery of new requirements. Customers should engage the SAP account team to work internally with the development and product management teams.

 

  • SAP Secure Operations Map ensures smooth operation of SAP solutions by taking action proactively, before severe security problems occur.

 

 

Depending on use case, when the company is ready for transformational initiatives, use the tools and services available to customers:

  • SAP Value Lifecycle Manager for a value analysis, total costs of ownership calculation and more.

 

  • SAP Readiness Check conducts a detailed analysis of our existing SAP system to help plan our digital transformation. For example, SAP S/4HANA Conversion, SAP S/4HANA Upgrade, SAP BW/4HANA Conversion, SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling, SAP Customer Experience Solutions, SAP SuccessFactors Solutions, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Datasphere, SAP BW Bridge.

 

  • SAP Custom Code Analytics uses aggregated Custom Code Management (CCM) data from your SAP Solution Manager. The application gives an overview about the custom code footprint of the ABAP systems and provides insights about code quality and usage.

 

  • SAP Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services are included in the SAP Enterprise Support contract. After each service, you receive a service report with an executive summary, findings and a detailed action plan to mitigate risks or improve your situation.

 

  • SAP Customer Evolution Kit for cost-free engagement to accelerate digital transformation to the cloud.

 

  • SAP Signavio, Process Insights, discovery edition, the successor of Process Discovery and Pathfinder on Spotlight, can get innovation recommendations based on the usage of the current system.

 

  • SAP AppHaus and its network partners offer different engagement types. From discovering innovation opportunities like rethinking business processes and user interfaces to delivering scalable solutions.

 

  • SAP Cloud Appliance Library offers a quick and easy way to create SAP workloads in our cloud infrastructure.

 

  • SAP Digital Discovery and Assessment is used to plan the scope of a planned SAP S/4HANA project (for SAP partners only).
  • SAP Simplification Item Catalogue helps better prepare for the transition to SAP S/4HANA and SAP BW/4HANA. It provides a description of all relevant changes that might have an impact when converting from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA or SAP BW to SAP BW/4HANA.

 

  • SAP Extensibility Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud covers a broad spectrum of topics that allow us to adapt standard business software to our business needs by leveraging the extensibility patterns and sample scenarios from SAP.

 

 

When customising is required (Clean Core), refer to the following frameworks and additional resources:

  • SAP Store is a digital marketplace with over 2000+ extensions where we can discover, try, and buy solutions from SAP partners.

 

  • SAP Partner Finder to find the best SAP partners to meet our needs.

 

  • SAP Cloud Application Programming Model helps build cloud-native applications with maximised productivity at minimised costs, based on proven best practices served out of the box.

 

  • SAP UI Development Toolkit for HTML5 is a platform for developing rich user interfaces for modern web business applications.

 

  • SAP Learning offers free online courses, tutorials, trials, and official SAP help documentation for anyone who wants to build in-demand SAP skills and prepare for SAP certifications.

 

  • SAP Community offers access to experts, knowledge sharing, the latest news, resources, and insights on SAP solutions and technologies.

 

  • SAP for Me is the digital companion to interact with SAP and aggregates important alerts, metrics, and insights about the SAP product portfolio with a single access point.

 

  • Microsoft Azure Architecture Centre on guidance for architecting solutions on Azure using established patterns and practices, including SAP on Azure (as Microsoft is our strategic partner).

 

I hope it also helps guide and drive your project. It is a starting point to prepare your colleagues to support business and tech decisions.

Surely I missed many helpful links here, so feel free to add yours in the comments too. Thanks!

Originally posted on LinkedIn.
